content.problemStatement:
You are given:


two arrays AAA and BBB, each consisting of NNN positive integers;
a positive integer KKK.
For a positive integer XXX, we say a set of indices {i1,i2,i3,‚Ä¶,im}\{i_1, i_2, i_3, \ldots, i_m\}{i1‚Äč,i2‚Äč,i3‚Äč,‚Ä¶,im‚Äč} (where 1‚Č§ij,m‚Č§N1 \leq i_j, m \leq N1‚Č§ij‚Äč,m‚Č§N) to be X-good if:

(Ai1(A_{i_1}(Ai1‚Äč‚Äč ‚ą£|‚ą£ Ai2A_{i_2}Ai2‚Äč‚Äč ‚ą£|‚ą£ Ai3A_{i_3}Ai3‚Äč‚Äč ‚ą£|‚ą£ ‚Ä¶\ldots‚Ä¶ ‚ą£|‚ą£ Aim)‚Č§XA_{i_m}) \leq XAim‚Äč‚Äč)‚Č§X, where ‚ą£|‚ą£ denotes the bitwise or operation.

The goodness of an X-good set of indices {i1,i2,i3,‚Ä¶,im}\{i_1, i_2, i_3, \ldots, i_m\}{i1‚Äč,i2‚Äč,i3‚Äč,‚Ä¶,im‚Äč} is defined as the sum of the corresponding elements from array BBB, i.e., Bi1+Bi2+Bi3+‚Ä¶+BimB_{i_1} + B_{i_2} + B_{i_3} + \ldots + B_{i_m}Bi1‚Äč‚Äč+Bi2‚Äč‚Äč+Bi3‚Äč‚Äč+‚Ä¶+Bim‚Äč‚Äč.

For each integer 1‚Č§X‚Č§K1 \leq X \leq K1‚Č§X‚Č§K, find out the total goodness over all possible X-good set of indices. Since this number can be huge, print it modulo 109+710^9+7109+7.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK ‚ÄĒ the number of elements in array AAA, and the parameter KKK.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,A3,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, A_3,\ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,A3‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč, the elements of array AAA.
The third line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers B1,B2,B3,‚Ä¶,BNB_1, B_2, B_3,\ldots,B_NB1‚Äč,B2‚Äč,B3‚Äč,‚Ä¶,BN‚Äč, the elements of array BBB.




Output Format
For each test case, output KKK space-separated integers on a new line, where the ithi^{th}ith integer (1‚Č§i‚Č§K)(1 \leq i \leq K)(1‚Č§i‚Č§K) denotes the total goodness over all possible i-good set of indices modulo 109+710^9+7109+7.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2‚čÖ1052\cdot 10^52‚čÖ105.
The sum of KKK over all test cases won't exceed 10610^6106.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: For each 1‚Č§X‚Č§K1 \leq X \leq K1‚Č§X‚Č§K:

For X=1:X=1:X=1: only good set of indices is {1}\{1\}{1}, hence the total goodness is B1=1B_1 = 1B1‚Äč=1.
For X=2:X=2:X=2: good set of indices are {1}\{1\}{1} and {2}\{2\}{2}, with goodness B1=1B_1=1B1‚Äč=1 and B2=2B_2=2B2‚Äč=2 respectively. Hence the total goodness is 333.
For X=3:X=3:X=3: good set of indices are {1}\{1\}{1} as (A1‚Č§3)(A_1 \leq 3)(A1‚Äč‚Č§3), {2}\{2\}{2} as (A2‚Č§3)(A_2 \leq 3)(A2‚Äč‚Č§3), {3}\{3\}{3} as (A1‚Č§3)(A_1 \leq 3)(A1‚Äč‚Č§3), {1,2}\{1, 2\}{1,2} as (A1(A_1(A1‚Äč ‚ą£|‚ą£ A2A_2A2‚Äč ‚Č§3)\leq 3)‚Č§3), {1,3}\{1, 3\}{1,3} as (A1(A_1(A1‚Äč ‚ą£|‚ą£ A3A_3A3‚Äč ‚Č§3)\leq 3)‚Č§3), {2,3}\{2, 3\}{2,3} as (A2(A_2(A2‚Äč ‚ą£|‚ą£ A3A_3A3‚Äč ‚Č§3)\leq 3)‚Č§3), {1,2,3}\{1, 2, 3\}{1,2,3} as (A1(A_1(A1‚Äč ‚ą£|‚ą£ A2A_2A2‚Äč ‚ą£|‚ą£ A3A_3A3‚Äč ‚Č§3)\leq 3)‚Č§3). Hence the total goodness is 1+2+2+(1+2)+(1+2)+(2+2)+(1+2+2)=201+2+2+(1+2)+(1+2)+(2+2)+(1+2+2)=201+2+2+(1+2)+(1+2)+(2+2)+(1+2+2)=20.

Test case 222: For each 1‚Č§X‚Č§K1 \leq X \leq K1‚Č§X‚Č§K:

For X=1:X=1:X=1: good set of indices are {1}\{1\}{1}, {2}\{2\}{2}, {1,2}\{1, 2\}{1,2}. Hence the total goodness is 1+2+(1+2)=61+2+(1+2)=61+2+(1+2)=6.
For X=2:X=2:X=2: good set of indices are {1}\{1\}{1}, {2}\{2\}{2}, {1,2}\{1, 2\}{1,2}. Hence the total goodness is 1+2+(1+2)=61+2+(1+2)=61+2+(1+2)=6.
